Emperor Cilipius was made governor and a bishop. He was a Christian who refused to accept baptism, and became an emperor after his death in 654.

After Cilius died, he left behind some of his sons (including Antigonopoulas) and some of his own daughters, but eventually made peace with the Roman authorities and took their place among his former allies.
